tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Afghan Refugee Becomes First Woman from Her Country to Summit Mount Everest
stories.summary: River Ahmad, who fled Afghanistan in 2018, became the first Afghan woman to summit Mount Everest in May, hoping to raise $200,000 for an online school for girls denied education under Taliban rule.

stories.headline: Displaced Lebanese Grandmother Bakes Hope: Feeding Thousands with Flatbread and Courage
stories.summary: Soubhiye Zeiter, 63, bakes mana'eesh flatbread each morning in a Beirut tent settlement, greeting dozens of neighbors and calling herself Om Mohammed while feeding her displaced community with warmth.

stories.headline: Paralyzed by ALS, Casey Harrell Speaks Through Brain Implant and Changes the World
stories.summary: Casey Harrell, paralyzed by ALS, has used his brain implant for over 3,800 hours to talk with his young daughter, work independently, and reclaim his voice after implants were embedded nearly three years ago.

stories.headline: Community Raises $600k for Elderly Woman Who Was Scammed and Went Hungry
stories.summary: A viral TikTok yard cleanup sparked a wave of generosity that raised over $600,000 for an elderly woman who had been scammed and gone days without food.

stories.headline: Journeyman Goaltender Brandon Bussi Leads Hurricanes to First Stanley Cup in 20 Years
stories.summary: Brandon Bussi, a journeyman goaltender, led Carolina to their first Stanley Cup in 20 years after years as a backup, proving late bloomers can have their moment of glory.

stories.headline: At 40, Cape Verde's 'Granny' Vozinha Stuns Spain at the World Cup
stories.summary: At 40, Cape Verde's goalkeeper Vozinha, who turned professional in 2012, delivered a stunning 0-0 shutout against Spain in the World Cup, earning his nickname 'granny' as a child taunted by older boys.

stories.headline: Brain-Responsive Stimulation System Helps Parkinson's Patients Walk Freely Again
stories.summary: Scientists developed an adaptive brain stimulation system that detects each step within fractions of a second and adjusts in real time, helping Parkinson's patients walk freely and dramatically reducing falls.

stories.headline: Stranger Answers Hospital's Wrong Number and Drives Elderly Patient Home with Quiet Kindness
stories.summary: Richard Hayes, a local legend who shares his name with a patient's son-in-law, answered a hospital's wrong-number call and drove elderly Norman Camburn home, refusing payment and asking only that he 'give a hand where you can.'

stories.headline: Single CRISPR Treatment Reduces Hereditary Angioedema Attacks by 87%, Offers Hope for Genetic Cures
stories.summary: In a Phase III trial of 80 patients with hereditary angioedema, a single CRISPR treatment reduced attacks by 87%, marking the first in vivo gene-editing therapy to show such effectiveness and safety.

stories.headline: World's First Large-Scale 100% Hydrogen Engine Powers Spanish Grid
stories.summary: Wärtsilä successfully operated a massive pure hydrogen engine on Spain's national grid, proving clean, emissions-free industrial power is finally here.

stories.headline: Wetland Plant Once Endangered Recovers So Well It's Removed from Threatened Species List
stories.summary: The northeastern bulrush has recovered from just 13 populations in 1991 to 148 populations across eight states, leading the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service to remove it from the endangered species list.

stories.headline: Red-Headed Woodpeckers and Rare Wildflowers Return to Ontario's Restored Prairies
stories.summary: After two decades of restoration, the Nature Conservancy of Canada has brought Red-headed Woodpeckers and rare wildflowers back to Ontario's Rice Lake Plains, rebuilding a vital tallgrass prairie ecosystem.

stories.headline: Ancient Lake Sturgeon Make a Comeback: Scientists Celebrate Reintegration Success
stories.summary: Encouraging signs emerge as researchers successfully reintroduce lake sturgeon to their historic waters, proving these prehistoric fish can recover.
